overview.description: Emerson is a quiet, family-oriented borough in the northwestern part of Bergen County. With tree-lined streets, well-maintained homes, and a strong school system, Emerson embodies classic suburban living. The borough was carved from Washington Township in 1903 and named after author Ralph Waldo Emerson. Its small-town character, combined with easy access to Paramus shopping and NYC commuter routes, makes it an appealing place for families.
highlights: Highly rated public school system with small class sizes
Charming small-town atmosphere with active community involvement
Close proximity to Paramus shopping centers
Access to the Saddle River County Park for recreation
Strong property values with well-maintained residential neighborhoods

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Emerson's housing stock consists primarily of single-family homes built from the 1950s through 1990s.
realEstate.recentTrends: Emerson's real estate market is competitive, with homes selling quickly due to the school system and family-friendly environment. Prices have risen steadily, and renovated or new-construction homes command significant premiums.
neighborhoodNotes: The central area near Kinderkamack Road features a mix of Cape Cods, colonials, and split-levels from the 1950s-1970s
Newer developments on the east side offer contemporary homes with modern amenities
The Saddle River border area features larger lots with a more rural feel
Properties near the commercial district on Kinderkamack Road offer walkability to shops and restaurants

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood — check the borough website
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected biweekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately on designated weeks
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Yard waste collected in spring and fall in biodegradable bags

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water) and sewer service is managed by the borough through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges appear on quarterly utility bills
Water quality reports published annually
Report water emergencies to the DPW or Veolia

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Emerson Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural work, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC modifications
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use
Applications submitted at Borough Hall on Linwood Avenue

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Emerson manages municipal parking along Kinderkamack Road and in small municipal lots.
details: Free parking available in municipal lots near the commercial district
Street parking along Kinderkamack Road has time limits during business hours
Overnight street parking prohibited during winter months (November-April)
NJ Transit commuter parking available at nearby stations

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers youth sports, summer camps, and community programs.
details: Youth baseball, softball, soccer, basketball, and lacrosse leagues
Summer recreation program for children
Adult programs and fitness classes
Community events and seasonal celebrations

additionalServices.title: Library Services
additionalServices.description: The Emerson Public Library provides a wide range of resources, programs, and community services.
details: Located at 20 Palisade Avenue
Children's story times, reading programs, and summer reading challenge
Adult book clubs, computer access, and reference services
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Emerson enforces noise restrictions to maintain the quiet residential character of the borough.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Power equipment (leaf blowers, mowers) restricted to daytime hours
Excessive noise from parties or gatherings subject to police response

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed and maintained
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 24 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles on properties
Trees and shrubs must not obstruct sidewalks or sight lines at intersections
Fences require a permit and must comply with setback and height regulations

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and kept under control at all times.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Barking dog complaints may result in fines

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Emerson enforces parking regulations on residential and commercial streets.
keyRules: No overnight street parking from November 1 through April 1
Time-limited parking on Kinderkamack Road during business hours
No parking within 25 feet of intersections or crosswalks
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required at all times
Commercial vehicles prohibited from parking on residential streets overnight

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Emerson regulates rental properties to ensure safety and habitability.
keyRules: Landlords must register rental properties with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Fire safety inspections required for multi-family dwellings
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Emerson Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Tree Removal
other.summary: Emerson has a tree preservation ordinance regulating removal of mature trees.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees above a certain diameter on residential property
Replacement tree planting may be required
Street trees are borough property and may not be trimmed or removed by residents
Dead or hazardous tree removal requests handled by the DPW

publicSchools.name: Emerson Junior-Senior High School
publicSchools.notes: Combined junior-senior high school offering AP courses, strong music program, and competitive athletics

publicSchools.name: Memorial School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school with small class sizes and active PTA

publicSchools.name: Patrick M. Villano School
publicSchools.notes: Intermediate school bridging elementary and middle school years

privateSchools.name: Assumption Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school affiliated with the Church of the Assumption, serving Emerson and surrounding communities

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey — Mahwah (12 miles)

whyMoveHere: Excellent public schools with small class sizes and strong community involvement
Quiet, safe, family-oriented neighborhoods with well-maintained homes
Minutes from Paramus shopping malls and major retail centers
Access to Saddle River County Park for outdoor recreation
Strong property values and stable real estate market

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes by bus; 30-60 minutes by car
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit buses run from nearby Oradell and Westwood to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Car commute via Route 4 to the George Washington Bridge. Many residents also use the NJ Transit train from nearby Oradell or Westwood stations on the Pascack Valley Line.

costOfLiving.summary: Emerson's cost of living is above the national average, consistent with Bergen County suburbs, with property taxes being the largest component.

pros: Top-rated schools with a strong sense of community
Safe, quiet neighborhoods ideal for families
Close to Paramus shopping and entertainment
cons: Higher property taxes typical of Bergen County
Limited commercial or nightlife options within the borough
No direct train station — requires driving to nearby stations
Small-town retail limited to Kinderkamack Road corridor
Housing prices have risen significantly, pricing out some buyers

restaurants.name: Emerson Tap House
restaurants.description: Popular neighborhood gastropub offering craft beers on tap, elevated pub fare, and a lively weekend atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Antica Pizzeria
restaurants.description: Neighborhood Italian restaurant serving Neapolitan-style pizza, fresh pasta, and classic Italian dishes.

restaurants.name: Sichuan Dynasty
restaurants.description: Authentic Chinese restaurant specializing in Sichuan cuisine with bold flavors and generous portions.

restaurants.name: Emerson Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with a full menu of American comfort food favorites.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park — Emerson Section
parks.description: A scenic section of the linear Bergen County park along the Saddle River, with walking trails, bicycle paths, and picnic areas.

parks.name: Borough Park
parks.description: Community park with playgrounds, ball fields, basketball courts, and a walking path, serving as the hub of Emerson recreation.

parks.name: Linwood Park
parks.description: Neighborhood pocket park with a playground and open green space for family recreation.

events.name: Emerson Fun Day
events.description: Annual community celebration featuring games, rides, food vendors, and live entertainment for all ages.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual tree lighting ceremony at Borough Hall with holiday music, hot chocolate, and a visit from Santa.

events.name: Emerson 5K Run/Walk
events.description: Community 5K race through the borough's streets benefiting local charities and school programs.

attractions.name: Emerson Public Library
attractions.description: Active community library hosting author visits, book clubs, children's programs, and cultural events.

attractions.name: Saddle River Bike Path
attractions.description: Multi-use path running through Emerson along the Saddle River, ideal for cycling, jogging, and walking.

shoppingAreas: Kinderkamack Road — Main commercial corridor with local shops, restaurants, and services
Paramus Shopping District (nearby) — Garden State Plaza, Paramus Park, Bergen Town Center, and Westfield Garden State Plaza

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Emerson Borough Hall, 99 Linwood Avenue, Emerson, NJ 07630

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
